title: The Groaning Bridge of {city.name}
trigger: Engineers warn that the main bridge near {city.landmark} is groaning audibly and dropping bolts into the river.
description: The bridge handles 40% of the city's freight. Closing it starves the {city.primary_industry_workers}, but keeping it open risks a mass casualty event. The {city.government_body} is currently debating the definition of imminent.

options.label: Close the bridge immediately for emergency repairs.
options.advisor_reason: Shut it down. A dead economy is better than dead bodies — and lawsuits will cost us triple.

consequences.follow_up: Traffic is a nightmare and the economy stalls, but nobody dies. The repairs take three times longer than promised.
consequences.city_reaction: Guess I live on this side of the river now. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Keep it open but ban heavy trucks and monitor closely.
options.advisor_reason: Fifty million is cheap. Let the trucks risk the detour — my logistics say one fall won't sink us.

consequences.follow_up: A truck ignores the ban and falls through a small hole. The bridge survives, but the lawsuit is massive.
consequences.city_reaction: Playing Russian roulette with my commute. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Duct tape the cracks and declare it a historic pedestrian walkway.
options.advisor_reason: It saves the budget and the freight industry was overrated anyway. Walkable cities are trendy.

consequences.follow_up: Hipsters love the new walkway until a strong wind blows a section away. The freight industry collapses entirely.
consequences.city_reaction: Very rustic, very deadly. {city.slang_reaction}.

title: Lights Out in {city.name}
trigger: The aging power grid fails during peak hours, plunging half of the city into darkness.
description: The utility company blames unprecedented demand despite it being a mild Tuesday. Hospitals are running on backup generators that have not been serviced in years. {city.media_outlet} is broadcasting via candlelight.

options.label: Implement strict rolling blackouts and buy expensive emergency power.
options.advisor_reason: Fair misery for everyone keeps the mob at bay. Two fifty hurts but the grid won't burn.

consequences.follow_up: Everyone gets power for four hours a day. It is miserable but fair. The budget is obliterated.
consequences.city_reaction: Candle sales are through the roof. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Cut power to residential slums to keep the business district lit.
options.advisor_reason: The slums don't pay taxes. Fifty million keeps the CBD humming — that's simple math.

consequences.follow_up: The {city.primary_industry_workers} riot in the dark. The business district looks beautiful, though.
consequences.city_reaction: Glad the empty office buildings have AC while we sweat. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Nationalize the utility company using armed police.
options.advisor_reason: A hundred fifty million and a CEO in cuffs sends a message. Let the cops learn hydropower.

consequences.follow_up: The CEO is arrested. The police have no idea how to run a power plant. The grid catches fire.
consequences.city_reaction: Seizing the means of electrocution. {city.slang_reaction}.

title: The Purple Tap Water of {city.name}
trigger: Tap water in three districts turns a vibrant glowing shade of purple.
description: The {city.media_outlet} reports it smells like sulfur and bubblegum. The {city.government_body} insists it is safe to drink if boiled for exactly 47 minutes. Citizens are panic-buying all available bottled water.

options.label: Distribute emergency bottled water and flush the entire system.
options.advisor_reason: One fifty for clear water and quiet citizens. Expensive, but panic costs more.

consequences.follow_up: The water runs clear after a week, but the city reserves are drained buying bottled water for everyone.
consequences.city_reaction: Showering with a water bottle is a humbling experience. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Claim the purple color is a harmless added mineral supplement.
options.advisor_reason: Thirty million and a lie. Purple teeth are tomorrow's problem — today we save the reserve.

consequences.follow_up: People's teeth start turning purple. A massive class-action lawsuit is filed against the city.
consequences.city_reaction: My dog drank it and now he glows in the dark. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Bottle the purple water and sell it as a novelty energy drink.
options.advisor_reason: Fifty million and a profit margin. The FDA raids us, but that's next quarter's logistics.

consequences.follow_up: It becomes a viral trend. The FDA raids city hall. The city made a small profit before the ban.
consequences.city_reaction: Tastes like pennies and regret, but I feel energized. {city.slang_reaction}.

title: Falling Ceilings in {city.name}
trigger: The ceiling of a major public school collapses overnight, displacing 3,000 students.
description: An inspection reveals the contractor used painted cardboard instead of drywall. Furious parents are demanding the city pay for private tutors. The {city.government_body} suggests outdoor learning.

options.label: Rent commercial office space as temporary classrooms.
options.advisor_reason: Two hundred million buys cubicles and calm parents. Depressing beats dangerous.

consequences.follow_up: The kids are learning in abandoned cubicles. It is depressing but safe. The smell of old coffee helps somehow.
consequences.city_reaction: My 8-year-old asked for a promotion today. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Force students to share facilities with the {city.primary_industry_workers}.
options.advisor_reason: Thirty million and the forklift certs practically write themselves. Cross-training is efficiency.

consequences.follow_up: Child labor laws are technically violated as kids learn practical skills on the factory floor.
consequences.city_reaction: Timmy failed spelling but he can operate a forklift now. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Declare the collapsed school an archaeological dig site for history class.
options.advisor_reason: Thirty million for tetanus and grit. Kids these days need character — and cheap field trips.

consequences.follow_up: Students dig through rubble for their own textbooks. Parents are not amused. Several contract tetanus.
consequences.city_reaction: Building character by dodging falling debris. {city.slang_reaction}.

title: The Filth Moat of {city.name}
trigger: A blocked main causes raw sewage to geyser out of manholes in a major district.
description: Residents are trapped in their homes by a moat of filth. Those affected are threatening to withhold all taxes until it is fixed. The {city.media_outlet} is running aerial footage of floating cars.

options.label: Divert all city sanitation crews to the affected district immediately.
options.advisor_reason: One hundred million to unblock one moat. Let the other districts stew — this stops a tax revolt.

consequences.follow_up: The district is saved, but the rest of the city's trash piles up for weeks. The inequality is glaring.
consequences.city_reaction: Glad some of us don't have to smell our own problems anymore. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Tell the affected residents to hire private plumbers.
options.advisor_reason: Thirty million and zero city crews. They'll cave — nobody holds out in raw sewage for long.

consequences.follow_up: Residents refuse to pay their taxes. The situation festers for days before private crews finally arrive.
consequences.city_reaction: Pay your own way through the sewage. Classic governance. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Pump the sewage into the {city.landmark} reservoir to contain it.
options.advisor_reason: Eighty million to ruin a landmark is a steal. Tourists vomit, but the sewage leaves the street.

consequences.follow_up: The landmark is ruined. Tourists vomit on arrival. The city becomes an international laughingstock.
consequences.city_reaction: Well it is definitely a landmark now. A smelly one. {city.slang_reaction}.

title: Grounded in {city.name}
trigger: The municipal airport's radar system catches fire, grounding all flights indefinitely.
description: Thousands of angry travelers are sleeping on the floor. The replacement part is stuck in customs, which is also run by the {city.government_body}. The {city.media_outlet} is interviewing furious tourists.

options.label: Expedite the customs process and pay overtime for installation.
options.advisor_reason: One fifty to bribe ourselves into efficiency. Tourists are loud, but grounded planes are louder.

consequences.follow_up: The radar is fixed but the delay cost the city millions in lost tourism revenue.
consequences.city_reaction: Bureaucracy so bad we have to bribe ourselves. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Use binoculars and walkie-talkies to land the planes manually.
options.advisor_reason: Thirty million and a prayer. The FAA yells, but we keep the runways moving by eyeball.

consequences.follow_up: Several near-misses occur. The FAA threatens to shut the airport down permanently.
consequences.city_reaction: Air traffic control by vibes. Terrifying. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Convert the grounded planes into expensive stationary airport hotels.
options.advisor_reason: Fifty million and the airlines sue. But tourist dollars for a middle seat? Genius logistics.

consequences.follow_up: Tourists actually pay for this. The airlines sue the city for unauthorized use of their aircraft.
consequences.city_reaction: Charging rent for a middle seat. Capitalism wins again. {city.slang_reaction}.

title: The Abyss of {city.name}
trigger: A sinkhole disguised as a pothole completely swallows a city bus near {city.landmark}.
description: Fortunately the bus was empty, but it is currently stuck vertically in the asphalt. The {city.government_body} wants to declare it a modern art installation to save removal costs.

options.label: Hire a heavy crane to remove it and fill the hole properly.
options.advisor_reason: One fifty to crane a bus out of the abyss. Weeks of closure, but we fix the road right.

consequences.follow_up: The bus is salvaged but the road remains closed for weeks while a proper investigation occurs.
consequences.city_reaction: Only took a whole bus disappearing to fix the road. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Leave the bus and pave over the top of it.
options.advisor_reason: Thirty million and a new speed bump. Who needs a crane when asphalt hides everything?

consequences.follow_up: The road has a weird bus-shaped hump in it now. Cars scrape their undercarriages daily for years.
consequences.city_reaction: The new speed bump looks suspiciously like public transit. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Declare it a subway entrance and charge people to climb into the bus.
options.advisor_reason: Thirty million and we finally have a subway. Tourists pay to smell diesel — that's revenue.

consequences.follow_up: Tourists actually pay for this. The subway smells like diesel and existential dread.
consequences.city_reaction: We finally got a subway system. {city.slang_reaction}.

title: Green Means Go in {city.name}
trigger: A hacker turns all traffic lights in the city green simultaneously.
description: The resulting gridlock is visible from space. The {city.primary_industry_workers} are losing millions by the hour as logistics grind to a halt. The {city.government_body} IT department cannot find the admin password.

options.label: Pay the hacker's ransom to restore the system.
options.advisor_reason: One hundred million to a teenager with a laptop. Humiliating, but cheaper than a full rebuild.

consequences.follow_up: The lights work again, but the city just negotiated with a teenager who was bored on a Saturday.
consequences.city_reaction: Defeated by a kid with a laptop. Inspiring. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Send police to manually direct traffic at every single intersection.
options.advisor_reason: Two hundred million for cop overtime. Exhausted and furious, but the traffic moves — eventually.

consequences.follow_up: The police are exhausted and furious. Traffic moves at a glacial pace. Crime spikes elsewhere in the city.
consequences.city_reaction: Cops doing useful work for once. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Turn off all traffic lights entirely and declare the city a shared space zone.
options.advisor_reason: Thirty million and Mad Max. Fistfights replace insurance claims — that's a win for my ledger.

consequences.follow_up: Mad Max rules apply. Fender benders are resolved via fistfights. Insurance companies flee the city.
consequences.city_reaction: Yield to the biggest vehicle. Those are the rules now. {city.slang_reaction}.
